cards.prompt: The capital of France is Paris.
cards.explanation: Paris is the capital and largest city of France.

cards.prompt: The Nile is a river in Africa.
cards.explanation: The Nile flows through north-eastern Africa and is one of the world's longest rivers.

cards.prompt: Australia is both a country and a continent.
cards.explanation: Australia is a single country that also forms its own continent.

cards.prompt: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.
cards.explanation: The Pacific is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceans.

cards.prompt: Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level.
cards.explanation: At about 8,849 metres, Everest is the highest mountain above sea level, in the Himalayas.

cards.prompt: Japan is an island country in Asia.
cards.explanation: Japan is made up of many islands off the east coast of Asia.

cards.prompt: The capital of the United States is New York City.
cards.explanation: The capital of the United States is Washington, D.C. New York is its largest city.

cards.prompt: The Amazon River is in Europe.
cards.explanation: The Amazon River is in South America, not Europe.

cards.prompt: Africa is the smallest continent.
cards.explanation: Africa is the second-largest continent; Australia/Oceania is the smallest.

cards.prompt: The Sahara Desert is located in South America.
cards.explanation: The Sahara Desert is in northern Africa.

cards.prompt: Russia is smaller in area than Italy.
cards.explanation: Russia is the largest country in the world by area, far larger than Italy.

cards.prompt: The Atlantic Ocean is larger than the Pacific Ocean.
cards.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is larger than the Atlantic Ocean.

cards.prompt: The capital of Japan is Tokyo.
cards.explanation: Tokyo is Japan's capital and one of the most populous metropolitan areas in the world.

cards.prompt: The capital of Australia is Sydney.
cards.explanation: Australia's capital is Canberra; Sydney is its largest city.

cards.prompt: Brazil is the largest country in South America.
cards.explanation: Brazil covers nearly half of South America, making it the continent's largest country by area and population.

cards.prompt: The Statue of Liberty is located in London.
cards.explanation: The Statue of Liberty stands in New York Harbor in the United States; it was a gift from France.

cards.prompt: The capital of Canada is Toronto.
cards.explanation: Canada's capital is Ottawa; Toronto is the country's largest city.

cards.prompt: The equator divides the Earth into the Northern and Southern Hemispheres.
cards.explanation: The equator is an imaginary line around the middle of the Earth that separates the Northern Hemisphere from the Southern Hemisphere.

cards.prompt: Canada shares a land border with the United States.
cards.explanation: Canada and the United States share the world's longest international land border.

cards.prompt: The capital of Brazil is Rio de Janeiro.
cards.explanation: Brazil's capital is Brasília, a planned city that replaced Rio de Janeiro as capital in 1960.

cards.prompt: The capital of Italy is Rome.
cards.explanation: Rome is Italy's capital and largest city, famous for its long history.

cards.prompt: Mount Kilimanjaro is located in Europe.
cards.explanation: Kilimanjaro is in Tanzania and is the highest mountain in Africa.

cards.prompt: The Arctic Ocean is the warmest ocean on Earth.
cards.explanation: The Arctic is the coldest and smallest of the world's oceans, largely covered by sea ice.

cards.prompt: Antarctica is the coldest continent on Earth.
cards.explanation: Antarctica holds the record for the lowest temperatures ever measured on Earth and is covered by a thick ice sheet.

cards.prompt: Switzerland has a long ocean coastline.
cards.explanation: Switzerland is a landlocked country in the Alps with no coastline at all.

cards.prompt: Egypt is a country in Africa.
cards.explanation: Egypt lies in the north-east corner of Africa, with a small part of its territory, the Sinai Peninsula, in Asia.

cards.prompt: The Alps are a mountain range in Europe.
cards.explanation: The Alps stretch across several European countries, including France, Switzerland, Italy, and Austria.

cards.prompt: The capital of South Korea is Busan.
cards.explanation: South Korea's capital is Seoul; Busan is the country's second-largest city and main port.

cards.prompt: Greenland is the largest island in the world.
cards.explanation: Greenland is the world's largest island; Australia is bigger but is counted as a continent.

cards.prompt: England and France are connected by a bridge.
cards.explanation: There is no bridge between them; they are linked by the Channel Tunnel, an undersea rail tunnel beneath the English Channel.

cards.prompt: The Andes mountain range is in South America.
cards.explanation: The Andes run along the western edge of South America and form the longest mountain range on land.

cards.prompt: Madagascar is an island off the coast of India.
cards.explanation: Madagascar lies in the Indian Ocean off the south-east coast of Africa, not near India.

cards.prompt: The shore of the Dead Sea is the highest point on Earth's land surface.
cards.explanation: The Dead Sea shore is the lowest land point on Earth, more than 400 metres below sea level.

cards.prompt: The Mediterranean Sea lies between Europe and Africa.
cards.explanation: The Mediterranean separates southern Europe from northern Africa and connects to the Atlantic through the Strait of Gibraltar.

cards.prompt: New Zealand is part of the continent of Asia.
cards.explanation: New Zealand is an island country in Oceania, in the south-western Pacific Ocean.

cards.prompt: The Great Barrier Reef is located off the coast of Australia.
cards.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef, the world's largest coral reef system, lies off the north-east coast of Australia.
